name: EPIPHYSEOLYSIS OF FEMORAL HEAD
definition: SLIPPED CAPITAL FEMORAL EPIPHYSIS atraumatic fracture through hypertrophic zone of physeal plate displaced by < ⅓ of metaphyseal diameter displaced by > ²/³ of metaphyseal diameter

demographics.Etiology: growth spurt, renal osteodystrophy, rickets, childhood irradiation, growth hormone
demographics.Mean age: 13 years for often overweight boys (range, 8-17 years), 11 years for girls; M÷F =
demographics.Location: usually unilateral; bilateral in 20-37% (at initial presentation in 9-18%)

dominant_finding: widening of epiphyseal growth plate (preslip phase):
distribution: usually unilateral; bilateral in 20-37% (at initial presentation in 9-18%)
discriminating_features: widening of epiphyseal growth plate (preslip phase):
decrease in neck-shaft angle with alignment change of growth plate to a more vertical

general: widening of epiphyseal growth plate (preslip phase):
decrease in neck-shaft angle with alignment change of growth plate to a more vertical
line of Klein (= line drawn along superior edge of femoral neck) fails to intersect the
epiphysis appears smaller ← posterior slippage: early slips are best seen on cross-table
sclerosis + irregularity of widened physis (chronic slip):
metaphyseal "blanch" sign = area of increased opacity in proximal part of metaphysis
